metadata.name: hmd-entity-storage
metadata.summary: basic storage library for entities and entity definitions

functions.name: hmd_entity_storage.engines.base_engine.gen_new_key
documentation:
Generate a new unique primary key.

This method is intended to create a unique integer primary key that
has a fairly large range of values.

```
return

Primary key.
```

methods.name: hmd_entity_storage.engines.dynamodb_engine.DynamoDbEngine._do_search_entities
documentation:
Search and filter items.

Retrieves values from the primary index or a specified secondary index. Items
are retrieved based on both keys and attribute filters.

```
param keys

A Dict of key name/value pairs to restrict the search.

param search_filter

A Dict of field name/value pairs to restrict the search.

param index

The name of the index to search. Defaults to the primary index.

return

List of the raw search results.
```

methods.name: hmd_entity_storage.engines.dynamodb_engine.DynamoDbEngine._do_freeze_current_rel_nouns
documentation:
Freeze the current version of the from and to nouns of the relationship.

```
param id

The relationship id.

return

```

functions.name: hmd_entity_storage.alembic.env.run_migrations_offline
documentation:
Run migrations in 'offline' mode.

This configures the context with just a URL
and not an Engine, though an Engine is acceptable
here as well.  By skipping the Engine creation
we don't even need a DBAPI to be available.

Calls to context.execute() here emit the given string to the
script output.
other values from the config, defined by the needs of env.py,
can be acquired:
my_important_option = config.get_main_option("my_important_option")
... etc.
password = os.environ.get("HMD_PASSWORD", "password")
host = os.environ.get("HMD_HOST", "localhost")
config.set_main_option(
"sqlalchemy.url", f"postgres://hmdroot:{password}@{host}/hmd_entities"

functions.name: hmd_entity_storage.alembic.env.run_migrations_online
documentation:
Run migrations in 'online' mode.

In this scenario we need to create an Engine
and associate a connection with the context.

functions.name: hmd_entity_storage.engines.gremlin_engine.backoff_initialize_connection
documentation: A method used by the backoff decorator to reset the connection

methods.name: hmd_entity_storage.engines.gremlin_engine.GremlinEngine._get_g
documentation:
Return the Gremlin graph traversal.
If self.with_strategies is True, then we add the necessary SubgraphStrategy to filter out is_deleted=True vertices and edges to the Python client traversal
If self.with_strategies is False, we return the bare traversal and assume the Gremlin Server graph was configured with the strategy.

classes.name: hmd_entity_storage.auditor.engine_auditor.AuditedEngine
documentation:
Wraps calls to supplied BaseEngine with AuditContext to track actions on Entities.

Args:
    engine (BaseEngine): storage engine derived from BaseEngine to audit
    engine_type (str): type of storage engine being wrapped, e.g. postgres
    audit_record_engine (BaseEngine): storage engine used to save audit records
    audit_record_class (Type[Noun]): HMD Noun class used to create audit records
    audit_relationship_class (Type[Relationship]): HMD Relationship class used to create audit record relationships
    prepare_audit_data (Callable): function to prepare dictionary of data for audit_record_class
    exclude (List[str]): list of class names to exclude for auditing

classes.name: hmd_entity_storage.auditor.engine_auditor.EngineAuditor
